channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Amnesty condemns Libya's migrant crackdown and growing ties with EU
item.description: Systematic human rights violations are being allegedly committed against refugees and migrants in Libya, perpetuated with the active support of EU Member States.

item.title: Judge overturns Trump administration's subpoenas against Minnesota
item.description: A US federal judge on Monday dismissed subpoenas issued by the Trump administration against authorities of the state of Minnesota.

item.title: US-Iran talks kicks-off in Switzerland with EU sidelined
item.description: "The MoU provides an opportunity for regional stability and the full restoration of freedom of navigation and safe transit through the Strait of Hormuz."

item.title: Millions of oil barrels pass through Strait of Hormuz despite attempted blockage
item.description: Despite Iran's claims of closure, millions of oil barrels crossed the Strait of Hormuz this weekend. Shipping firms remain on alert as tensions rise and risks intensify.

item.title: US-Iran talks kick off despite attacks on Lebanon
item.description: As Iran threatens to shut the Strait of Hormuz, nuclear talks with the US face mounting tensions. Ceasefire violations in Lebanon further complicate fragile negotiations.

item.title: British foreign minister calls on Keir Starmer to step down
item.description: Starmer’s leadership under siege: resignation rumours grow as Labour MPs rally behind candidate Burnham.

item.title: Red Devils seek to bounce back after lacklustre start to World Cup
item.description: Belgium will play against Iran for the first time in their footballing history.

item.title: King Charles becomes first British monarch to share tax report
item.description: King Charles opens the books on his income as demands for scrutiny grow. The unprecedented tax report includes millions from the Duchy of Lancaster and private assets.

item.title: 300 participants form human chain on World Refugee Day in Brussels
item.description: Refugees share harrowing stories as activists mark World Refugee Day, spotlighting systemic gaps in asylum protections.

item.title: Flemish-Brazilian missionary on path to beatification
item.description: A Flemish-born priest who dedicated his life to Brazil has been declared venerable, marking a major step toward sainthood.

item.title: Thousands of Belgian Iranians join large Paris protests
item.description: Around 50,000 people had travelled to Paris for the demonstration.

item.title: UN expresses concerns over 'migrant return hubs'
item.description: The UN warns that new EU migrant rules could violate human rights. Plans to outsource deportation of rejected asylum seekers raise serious concerns.

item.title: One in ten unemployed who lost benefits in recent reforms has found work
item.description: Some 100,000 people have lost their unemployment benefits, with more waves due later this year and in 2027.

item.title: Ambassador welcomes 'mutual affection' between Belgium and Japan
item.description: Japan’s emperor returns to Belgium for first state visit in 30 years, celebrating 160 years of diplomatic ties between two nations with shared values.

item.title: Japan imperial couple visit Belgium
item.description: Emperor Naruhito’s study of rivers shapes a unique visit to Belgium, linking diplomacy, history, and cutting-edge research.

item.title: Israel says it is striking Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli airstrikes overnight from Thursday to Friday in southern Lebanon's Nabatieh region have killed at least 16 people.

item.title: After Iran, Trump wants to focus on North Korea, says South Korean president
item.description: Hours after announcing the Iran deal, Donald Trump posted an uncaptioned photo of his 2018 meeting with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un in Singapore.

item.title: FIFA removes nearly 400,000 hateful comments on social media
item.description: "We’re trying to educate young people so that they grow up to be empathetic individuals, not just people who love the sport. There is no place for discrimination in our society."

item.title: US and Iran sign provisional agreement to end war
item.description: The 'memorandum of understanding' stipulates that the Strait of Hormuz will be reopened and that all conflicts in the region will be brought to an end.

item.title: Decathlon offers €2,000 worth of free shares to employees worldwide
item.description: Decathlon employs around 103,000 people globally and operates 1,902 stores worldwide.
